extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Caden won sprint after crosswind anxiety kept peloton compressed all day, YATES Simon caught out losing time despite MATTHEWS Michael leadout
tactical_patterns: BikeExchange split sprint duties: MATTHEWS stayed front in crosswind threat while entire team except GROVES paced YATES back.
INEOS drove tempo hard with GC depth defending against crosswinds rather than letting specialists control, revealing overall prioritization.
Crosswind anxiety compressed peloton causing crashes rather than creating actual splits, showing psychological impact exceeded tactical reality.
GROVES followed BAUHAUS wheel through final turns rather than trusting MATTHEWS leadout, showing adaptive sprint positioning.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: caught out by crosswind positioning despite team having race leader jersey and controlling tempo
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: provided leadout while wearing leader jersey after dropping back for YATES, showing dual-role capability
rider_signals.PORT Richie: negative: abandoned team duties due to illness, told teammates not to wait, signals poor form
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: neutral: isolated after Bahrain work finished but still took second, suggesting individual strength without team depth
rider_signals.KOSCIUSZKO Koen: negative: pulled back from front group to pace despite being viable sprint option, team tactics overrode personal opportunity
